pages.raw_text:
BOISE, May IA cottage
across the street from the court
house has been fixed upon as the
home for the Haywood jury. In or
der to get it a liberal bonus was
paid the family living there to va
cate for the period of the trial. The
house was selected because it is
close and convenient and will en-
able the jury to live in comfort.


Sheriff Shad Hodgin has both a
personal and official interest in this
matter or the jury. He served,
while back, as member of the
jury in criminal case, and he
knows what ''roughing it" means.
He intends that the men having to
stand the long and arduous siege
in this case shall have proper
degree of comfort. A cook will pre-
pare their meals at the cottage, and
every juror will sleep in bed The
great court house lawn will afford
them place for exercise.


The sheriff will adopt extraordi-
nary precautions to prevent a mis-
u iol thi puSiI even lIC dDpCdi dllSC
of misconduct on the part of the
jury, To that end special bailffs
will guard them day andnight.


A stairway has been constructed
at the rear of the court house, so
that the Jurors will not come in
contact with the crowd in the cor
ridOr of the building. They will
pass from the court room through
Judge Woods private Office, down
the special stairway and across the
lawn and the street to the cottage.


AS an instance of the care which
must be exercised, the sheriff
cites a case that recently fiured
in the courts. A bailiff was con-
Quoting jurors in a criminal case
through the streets to restaurant.
when one of the good men and
true stepped around the corner to
buy cigar There was no evil
dence that he had been approach
ed" in fact he had not been spoken
to about the case. but he had been
ont of the bailiff's sight and the
judge set aside the verdict.


In the matter of Haywood the
sheriff will guard against the
slightest slip that might give
ground for criticism.
